items.title: New Writers' Week boss says books, not political views, will inform line-up

children.content: The new chair of Adelaide Writers' Week has sent a statement to would-be critics, quipping that Pauline Hanson would be worthy of inclusion on a literary line-up if the One Nation leader were "secretly writing Australia's next best crime novel".

gallery.altText: A smiling woman leans an arm over a park bench

thumbnail.altText: A smiling woman leans an arm over a park bench

items.title: New grapevine virus GINV detected for first time in Australia

children.content: A grapevine virus known in China and Japan that affects plant growth and productivity has been detected in Australia for the first time in Victorian, Queensland and South Australian table grapes.

image.altText: Undergrowth of table grape vines.

items.title: Law firm ordered to pay more than $4.7m after overcharging outback council

children.content: The NSW Supreme Court has ordered a Sydney-based law firm and related companies to pay the Broken Hill City Council more than $4.7 million.

image.altText: picture of a sign saying 'supreme court'

items.title: Figure skating teen makes history for Australia

children.content: At just 15, Hana Bath became the first Australian female to ever land a triple axel in competition. The rising star now has her eye on the 2030 Winter Olympics.

gallery.altText: A figure skater wearing skates on ice holders her hands in the air

thumbnail.altText: A figure skater wearing skates on ice holders her hands in the air

items.title: 'Be honest with people': Politicians across the country react to NDIS cuts

children.content: State leaders are reacting to the sweeping cuts coming for the National Disability Insurance Scheme, with one minister arguing the federal government will "walk away" from those in need.

image.altText: Amanda Camm wearing a suit jacket and speaking into a microphone.

items.title: Woman faces murder charge over alleged hit-and-run that killed man

children.content: The accused's lawyer today told the court that her client was one of four young females in a car that was being attacked when she attempted to flee.

image.altText: A man puts an arm around a woman in front of neon lights and big glass windows

items.title: 'Unusual' warm weather sees Adelaide residents spending days at beach

children.content: Adelaide residents have been making the most of unusually warm weather for the end of April, with crowds flocking to the beach before the school holidays finish.

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: Lack of 24-hour health care in SA prisons questioned

children.content: Advocates call for improved medical resourcing in South Australia's prisons after a coronial inquest into the death of an inmate.

image.altText: Sign at the entrance to low security prison Cadell Training Centre

items.title: 'Very short on facts': Electoral Commission's bizarre communications in a crisis

children.content: As trust in their ability to effectively administer an election erodes by the day, so too seemingly does their transparency with the public.

image.altText: A woman holding a ballot walks past other people voting facing away in cardboard booths

items.title: Stump that created hole in footpath becomes neighbourhood attraction

children.content: Adelaide residents have decorated a large tree stump and then the hole it left in a footpath to highlight the local council's delay in removing it.

image.altText: A rotten tree stump surrounded by traffic cones, including one topped by a green hat

items.title: Alleged drink driver detected at 151km/h with child in ute, police say

children.content: A man who was allegedly drink driving with a child inside a speeding ute in Adelaide's south last night will face court after being stripped of his licence, police say.

image.altText: An SA Police logo.

items.title: Woman's life 'changed for the better' after GPs in SA trained to diagnose ADHD

children.content: About 100 South Australian GPs are now qualified to diagnose ADHD and provide treatment, with the hope of that number doubling by the end of the year, with one patient describing her diagnosis through her GP as "life-changing".

image.altText: A person standing in the Adelaide CBD.

images.altText: donald trump points his finger at a colleague, he is sat down and dressed in a navy suit
Smoke rises from a village in southern Lebanon as the Israeli army operates tanks in the area

articles.title: If ships are being fired upon and seized how can there be a ceasefire?

children.content: As part of its blockade of Iran, ships have been seized by US forces, others fired upon by Iran and, most recently, boarded by the regime's revolutionary guards. This is all while both sides maintain there is a ceasefire.

image.altText: Masked men in sand-coloured army fatigues climbing a ladder off the side of a ship.

articles.title: Indonesian minister suggests tax on another key shipping route

children.content: The suggestion of levying ships using the Strait of Malacca was suggested and then walked back by Indonesia's finance minister, but has prompted a response from Canberra.

image.altText: Dozens of cargo vessels stationed in a water way

children.content: Donald Trump says the US will work with Lebanon to "protect itself from Hezbollah" and that he will meet with Benjamin Netanyahu soon. Follow live.

items.title: Trump says ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel extended for three weeks

updates.title: Lebanese and Israeli ambassadors thank Trump for 'historic' moment
Ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel extended for three weeks, Trump says
IDF: Missile launches from Lebanon into northern Israel intercepted

items.title: 'Genuinely speechless': Appeal board's stunning claim in homophobia case

children.content: The appeal board in the Lance Collard homophobia case says homophobic and racist language is commonplace in the AFL due to the "highly competitive" nature of the game.

image.altText: Lance Collard stands with both arms in the air during a St Kilda vs West Coast Eagles AFL match in 2025.

items.title: 'They deserve death': Officer used dispatch system to monitor police wife

children.content: Text messages read in court set out how a Tasmania Police officer monitored his wife's movements, including via an internal dispatching system, as part of a 15-month family violence matter.

image.altText: A man seen in side profile view.

items.title: Mapping the five other shipping routes critical for the global economy

children.content: Oil prices have spiked since the Strait of Hormuz was closed. With about 90 per cent of all traded goods shipped by sea, what are the other key shipping lanes in the world?

articles.title: How Aussies near you are coping with the fuel crisis

children.content: Petrol prices are reshaping Australian life in ways big and small — from a grandmother's isolation to a farmer's future.

image.altText: Three people who are affected by high fuel prices.

articles.title: Fuel filter: What to expect on Australian fuel supply and pricing for the weekend

children.content: The week's most important fuel news, analysis and pricing information.

image.altText: Fuel gauge graphic with dollar sign

articles.title: Warnings of power price rises, missed renewable targets under proposed tax

children.content: The treasurer says changes to capital gains tax for foreign investors is a clarification, but experts say it could dampen investment.

image.altText: Wind turbines among clouds.

gallery.altText: A map of different international waterways across the world.

thumbnail.altText: A map of different international waterways across the world.

items.title: ASX 200 down after Wall Street losses, oil prices spike again

children.content: Oil prices are spiking again on global markets as the ASX begins the final day of the week in negative territory. It comes after the the Nasdaq shed almost 1 per cent. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Geelong refinery boss defends soaring fuel profit margins saying 'they need to make money'

children.content: The Viva Energy refinery provides about 10 per cent of Australia's fuel. Its chief executive, Scott Wyatt, chats to Alan Kohler about the company's future in the wake of the Geelong fire.

image.altText: Smoke rises off the refinery building at Corio

items.title: The legacy of once-banned soldiers that hides in plain sight

children.content: Thousands of Indian soldiers served alongside Anzac forces at Gallipoli, leaving their legacy in the "puggaree" — a traditional Indian headwrap that was adapted to adorn the slouch hats worn by the Australian army.

articles.title: VC recipient calls for federal government to step up veteran support

children.content: One of Australia's most decorated soldiers has called for a national rethink on the care of veterans and their families.

image.altText: A man holds a portrait of a young man in military uniform

articles.title: Which states get a public holiday for Anzac Day?

children.content: Anzac Day is observed on April 25 every year. This year that date falls on a Saturday, but that doesn't mean a long weekend for everyone.

image.altText: poppies on a cenotaph

articles.title: Regional RSLs facing closure as membership dwindles

children.content: One RSL sub-branch is down to just three members and is worried about succession, but it is not alone in its troubles.

image.altText: A man and a woman sitting at a table looking at a document.

items.title: 'This is now terminal': UK braces for prospect of sixth PM in seven years

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer, who led Labour to a landslide general election victory in July 2024, has become so unpopular that even some colleagues are framing his demise as a case of not if, but when.

image.altText: A man in a suit looking down

items.title: 'Finfluencers' suspected of giving unlawful advice hit with warning notices

children.content: The warning notice to the so-called "finfluencers" relates to suspected provision of unlicensed financial advice, including promoting claims of guaranteed returns.

image.altText: hands holding a phone as the stock market runs behind it

items.title: Jane Fonda was in her 40s when she changed the way we exercise

children.content: In the 1980s, the fitness industry as we know it was just getting started. One of the most important people in developing the way we exercise today was Jane Fonda, who released a best-selling aerobics video in 1982.

gallery.altText: Actress Jane Fonda in a yellow exercise outfit stretching with her left foot pointed upward in the air.

thumbnail.altText: Actress Jane Fonda in a yellow exercise outfit stretching with her left foot pointed upward in the air.

items.title: Is it ever OK to use someone else's toothbrush?

children.content: About half of us have shared a toothbrush — usually between spouses, or parents and children. We asked the experts if it's a good idea, even in an emergency.

image.altText: three toothbrushes sit in a glass

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's eleventh-hour announcement, a disabled bird and a speedy humanoid robot

children.content: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

image.altText: A red and black robot on a street with barriers in the background and a ribbon falling to the ground.

items.title: Hundreds of fishing boats sit idle off southern China — but they're not there to fish

children.content: Off China's southern coast, hundreds of fishing boats sit idle. The crews aren't there to fish, but rather to "maintain presence" as part of a shadowy, state-funded fleet.

gallery.altText: A satellite image giving a birdseye view of boats scattered across a bay.

thumbnail.altText: A satellite image giving a birdseye view of boats scattered across a bay.

items.title: Pentagon awards $276m AUKUS contract funded by Australia

children.content: The US Department of Defense announces the first major submarine contract to deliver on the AUKUS security deal.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

items.title: Prince Harry urges Putin to 'stop this war' on visit to Ukraine

children.content: Prince Harry makes a surprise visit to Kyiv, urging Russia's President Vladimir Putin to end the war and calling on US President Donald Trump to show leadership to help resolve the conflict in Ukraine.

image.altText: Britain's Prince Harry speaks at the Kyiv Security Forum, he is stood at a lectern behind a microphone

items.title: US watchdog announces Epstein files audit

children.content: The audit follows months of controversy over censorship, missed deadlines and the publication of survivors' details.

image.altText: ghislaine maxwell and jeffrey epstein seen together on a black background

items.title: US soldier charged with making $560,000 on Maduro removal bets

children.content: In the weeks leading up to Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro's January 3 capture, Gannon Ken Van Dyke used sensitive classified information to make wagers on prediction market Polymarket.

image.altText: Nicolas Maduro being perp walked by multiple heavily armed police.

items.title: Whale rescuers describe skill and courage needed to detangle 40-tonne marine mammal

children.content: As thousands of whales begin their annual northern migration up the east coast of Australia, teams of specialist rescuers are preparing to help them along the way.

gallery.altText: Aerial view of two boats on the water reaching towards a fake whale tail.

thumbnail.altText: Aerial view of two boats on the water reaching towards a fake whale tail.

items.title: Living on idyllic headland poses health problems for mob of kangaroos

children.content: Kangaroos lying on a NSW mid north coast headland may be a "selfie" dream for tourists, but some are becoming weak from malnutrition.

image.altText: three kangaroos on a beach in dune grass

items.title: Teen killer of Qld grandmother loses sentence appeal

children.content: The 16-year sentence handed to the teenager who murdered south east Queensland woman Vyleen White has been upheld by the state's Court of Appeal.

image.altText: a brunette woman wearing a white blazer

items.title: Hackers steal $3.7m from Sri Lanka earmarked for debts to Australia

children.content: Cyber criminals have hacked Sri Lanka's finance ministry, stealing millions of dollars destined to repay Australia.

image.altText: Cyberattack illustration

items.title: Italy dismisses 'shameful' idea to replace Iran at football World Cup

children.content: Italian officials say they are not interested in replacing Iran at the FIFA World Cup, after the idea was raised by a Trump ally.

image.altText: A man in a bright blue suit walks down the street.

items.title: Ancient fossils reveal when the iconic Twelve Apostles formed

children.content: An analysis of tiny fossils of sea creatures found in rock samples around the Port Campbell region in south-western Victoria has refined our understanding of how the Twelve Apostles formed.
